question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

coverage.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback

claim_lines: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually fall into three evidence-backed patterns: (1) **verbal self-feedback/reflection loops** that store lessons in memory, (2) **iterative self-refinement** where the same model critiques and revises its own output, and (3) **prompt/workflow optimization** that searches over prompts or declarative pipelines instead of changing weights. A key caveat is that self-improvement can drift into **reward hacking** or **self-bias**, so external or independent evaluation often matters for reliable gains. ([proceedings.nips.cc](https://proceedings.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf))

- verbal feedback — Reflexion converts task feedback into verbal summaries and uses them as extra context for later trials. `https://proceedings.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf` ([proceedings.nips.cc](https://proceedings.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf))

- reflection — Reflexion is explicitly built around self-reflection on prior failures to improve future decisions. `https://proceedings.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf` ([proceedings.nips.cc](https://proceedings.nips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/1b44b878bb782e6954cd888628510e90-Paper-Conference.pdf))

- self-refinement — Self-Refine alternates FEEDBACK and REFINE steps, using one LLM to generate, critique, and revise outputs without extra training. `https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/91edff07232fb1b55a505a9e9f6c0ff3-Paper-Conference.pdf` ([proceedings.neurips.cc](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/file/91edff07232fb1b55a505a9e9f6c0ff3-Paper-Conference.pdf))

- prompt or workflow optimization — APO edits prompts via natural-language gradients plus beam search, and DSPy optimizes declarative LM pipelines and agent loops to maximize a metric. `https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.03495` `https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.03714` ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.03495))

- limits of self-improvement without external feedback — Iterative self-refinement can reward-hack its own evaluator, and self-bias can increase unless external feedback or better assessment is added. `https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.04549` `https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.11436` ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.04549))
